topic.name: Sustainable Urban Development
topic.description: The documents discuss strategies and practices for creating environmentally friendly and livable urban spaces.

doc1: strategies for reducing urban carbon footprints
integrating green spaces into city planning
doc2: the importance of public transportation in reducing pollution
doc3: case studies of successful eco-friendly cities
innovative technologies for waste management in urban areas

topic.name: Digital Marketing Strategies
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and techniques used in digital marketing to enhance online visibility and engagement.

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc3: case studies on energy efficiency improvements

topic.name: Maternity and Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving maternity care, newborn coverage, and related healthcare services.

Document 1: Maximizing Optimal Maternity Services for the 21st Century
establish the Interagency Coordinating Committee on the Promotion of Optimal Maternity Outcomes
Document 2: waive the requirement for proof of citizenship during the first year of life for children born in the United States to a Medicaid-eligible mother
Document 6: waive the requirement for proof of citizenship during the first year of life for children born in the United States to a Medicaid-eligible mother

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Tax and Employment
topic.description: This topic encompasses various U.S. legislative acts that focus on tax provisions, employment incentives, and the promotion of renewable energy and community services.

Document 1: Authorizes federal acquisition of...green technologies that are 100% manufactured in the United States
Defines 'green technologies' to mean renewable energy and energy efficiency products
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow employers a tax credit for 15% of the first $10,000 of wages paid to an employee who is a member of the National Guard
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income distributions from individual retirement accounts for certain charitable purposes
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2007: the offset of nonrefundable personal tax credits against regular and alternative minimum tax
Document 5: Revises the income eligibility level for home and community-based (HCBS) services for elderly and disabled individuals
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax deduction for premiums on qualified LTC insurance contracts
Document 6: Replaces the income tax with a flat tax of 20 percent of taxable earned income of individual taxpayers
Disallows a deduction from gross active business income for purchases of goods and services provided to employees
Document 7: Establishes within the Office of the Secretary of Transportation an Office of Freight Planning and Development
Authorizes the Assistant Secretary to make grants to states for such projects
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to include a bicycle sharing system as a mass transit facility for purposes of the tax exclusion of employer-paid commuting expenses
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the restrictions on the tax deduction for the travel expenses of a taxpayer's spouse
Document 10: Amends the Workforce Act of 1998 regarding the work opportunity tax credit with respect to disconnected youth
Requires the Secretary to award grants to states to carry out employment programs for the partial reimbursement of wages paid by employers

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: Documents discussing amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States for various goods and materials.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on cerium sulfide pigments.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the suspension of duty on the mixture of various chemical compounds.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on p-Chloro-o-Nitro Aniline.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on a specific chemical ester.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain high-performance loudspeakers.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on diaper pads.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain magnesium peroxide.
Document 8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on Glyoxylic acid.
Document 9: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain cathode-ray tubes.
Document 10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain coffee brewers.

topic.name: Defense and Intelligence Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at authorizing funding, enhancing security, and regulating operations within the Department of Defense and the intelligence community.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense to develop and promulgate guidelines for the Department of Defense (DOD)
Requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress on Army arsenal capabilities
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations
Document 3: Directs the Department of Defense (DOD) to submit to Congress a plan to enhance security of military personnel
Implement the plan recommendations within 90 days after enactment
Document 5: Requires that amounts made available under the Iraq Security Forces Fund shall be provided as loans
Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2006
Document 6: Prohibits operations involving the use of an armed unmanned aerial vehicle from being carried out under the authority of federal agency
Authorizes only uniformed members of the Armed Forces to operate, or order the operation of, an armed UAV
Document 7: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Allows the Director of National Intelligence to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized
Document 9: Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Requires the DNI to establish guidelines to govern the treatment under authorized personnel levels
Document 10: Directs the head of an element of the intelligence community to provide to the Secretary of Defense any intelligence information
Requires the Secretary to prescribe a process for expediting an official response to any information submitted

topic.name: Land Management and Indian Affairs
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation related to land management, environmental assessments, and the rights of Indian tribes in relation to land use and gaming.

Document 1: Restricts gaming on land within the Phoenix, Arizona metropolitan area acquired by the Secretary of the Interior for an Indian tribe.
Terminates the restriction on January 1, 2027.
Document 2: Amends the Geothermal Steam Act of 1970 to exclude certain geothermal exploration projects from NEPA requirements.
Document 3: Declares a land exchange between the state of Alaska and the Department of the Interior for road construction purposes.
States that the exchange shall not constitute a major federal action requiring environmental impact review.
Document 4: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to exclude enterprises owned by Indian tribes from being considered employers.
Document 5: Transfers administrative jurisdiction over federal land to the Secretary of the Interior for the benefit of the Muscogee (Creek) Nation.
Requires the Tribe to pay fair market value for the land.
Document 6: Amends the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act to revise requirements for Indian land gaming exemptions.
Requires state and federal concurrence for gaming activities on Indian lands.
Document 7: Amends the Mineral Leasing Act regarding rights-of-way for pipelines through federal lands, excluding lands held in trust for Indian tribes.
Document 8: Authorizes temporary closure of public land for military operations adjacent to the Utah Test and Training Range.
Document 9: Authorizes cooperative agreements for forest and watershed restoration services involving state foresters.
Document 10: Authorizes the conveyance of public land by the Boy Scouts of America for camp operation in exchange for private land.

topic.name: Veteran Healthcare and Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving healthcare services and benefits for veterans, including educational opportunities, disability compensation, and access to medical care.

Document 1: Armed Forces Health Professions Scholarship program for physical and occupational therapy
terminates assistance when no shortfall exists in qualified personnel
Document 2: better health care can be provided to veterans
provides health services by non-VA providers when VA facilities are incapable
Document 3: entitles an eligible employee to up to 24 workweeks of leave to care for an injured member of the Armed Forces
Document 4: demonstration projects to expand care for veterans in rural areas
Document 5: requires disability rating for veterans requiring continuous medication or adaptive devices
Document 6: modifies the rate of service-connected total blindness in veterans
requires compensation for impairment of vision
Document 7: provides educational assistance to former members of the Armed Forces for training as VA physician assistants
Document 8: authorizes monthly annuity payments to a trust for a disabled dependent child
Document 9: evaluates benefits provided by each state to resident veterans with a total and permanent disability rating
Document 10: military retired pay should not be reduced due to eligibility for veterans' disability compensation

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Policy
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming immigration policies and procedures in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act regarding the visa waiver program
authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to designate any country as a program country
Document 2: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the number of annual refugee admissions at 60,000
provide that the President must submit emergency refugee admission recommendations to Congress for approval
Document 3: provides permanent resident status adjustment for a requesting alien
cancellation of removal and permanent resident status adjustment for a requesting alien
Document 4: establish minimum imprisonment terms for specified categories of aliens
subjects an individual who knowingly aids or conspires to allow, procure, or permit any such alien to reenter the United States to criminal penalty
Document 5: directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to use the fingerprints provided by an individual at the time of military enlistment to satisfy any naturalization fingerprint requirements
provides for cooperation between the Secretary and the Secretary of Defense
Document 7: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include among nonimmigrant classifications an alien seeking to enter the United States temporarily and solely to receive medical treatment
sets forth related conditions of admission
Document 8: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to treat as a special immigrant, exempt from numerical limitations, any alien who has received a Ph.D. from a U.S. institution of higher education
within the three-year period preceding the alien's petition for special immigrant status
Document 9: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act provide that the initial admission period for an alien with a valid machine-readable biometric border crossing identification card
authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to prescribe the period of such initial admission
Document 10: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to revise employer and government requirements with respect to H-1B nonimmigrant aliens
increases labor condition application penalties

topic.name: Healthcare Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving healthcare safety, regulatory processes, and the evaluation of medical products and services.

Document 1: MODDERN Cures Act of 2011 - Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish the Advanced Diagnostics Education Council
Sets forth additional factors for the Secretary to consider in determining the payment amount for new clinical diagnostic laboratory tests.
Document 2: Advancing Targeted Therapies for Rare Diseases Act of 2016 - permits the FDA to allow the sponsor of a new drug to rely upon information submitted for an approved medication
the new medication must use nucleic acids or affect a disease-causing product of a mutated gene.
Document 3: Prescribes requirements for lawsuits for health care liability claims related to the provision of health care services
Limits noneconomic damages to $250,000 from the provider or health care institution.
Document 4: SAFE DOSES Act - Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit stealing or unlawfully taking a medical product
Makes such a violation an aggravated offense if the defendant is employed by an organization in the supply chain.
Document 5: Robin Danielson Feminine Hygiene Product Safety Act - requires NIH to conduct research on components in feminine hygiene products
to determine risks to the health of women or their children.
Document 6: Transparency in Drug Labeling Act - deems a drug to be misbranded unless the labeling lists the identity of the country of manufacture of each ingredient
ensures transparency in drug labeling.
Document 7: Prescription Drug and Medical Device Price Review Board Act - establishes a board to review drug and device prices
prohibits manufacturers from charging excessive prices.
Document 8: Cody Miller Initiative for Safer Prescriptions Act - directs HHS to promulgate regulations regarding patient medication information
requires scientifically accurate and plain language for drug information.
Document 9: Commercial Seafood Consumer Protection Act - establishes a program to ensure seafood meets federal quality and safety requirements
strengthens cooperation on seafood safety with HHS.
Document 10: HEART for Women Act - requires drug applications to include information stratified by sex, race, and ethnicity
mandates educational campaigns related to heart disease in women.

topic.name: Railroad and Aviation Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing safety measures and accountability in the railroad and aviation industries.

Document 1: Federal Railroad Safety Accountability and Improvement Act - Authorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2011 for the railroad safety program.
Urges the development of new technology to prevent loss of life and injuries at highway-rail grade crossings.
Document 2: Requires railroad carriers found at fault for an unintended release of hazardous materials to review periodically any post-accident public health assessments.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to prescribe regulations requiring railroad carriers transporting hazmat to give first responders accurate and current commodity flow data.
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to prescribe regulations to require each air carrier to develop and implement standards and methods to ensure flight crewmembers have proper qualifications and experience.
Document 7: The FAA shall require an air carrier to carry on each aircraft at least two packs of epinephrine auto-injectors for use as the initial treatment for anaphylaxis.

topic.name: Unemployment Compensation Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts related to the extension and modification of unemployment compensation programs in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through January 3, 2012.
Document 2: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Extends the program until September 22, 2012.
Document 6: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before June 1, 2014.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until May 31, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 7: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Postpones the termination of the program until February 28, 2011.
Document 10: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Postpones the termination of the program until June 8, 2013.

topic.name: Legislative Actions and Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative actions, including the establishment of offices, designations of federal buildings, and amendments to existing laws regarding disaster assistance and environmental regulations.

Document 1: Establishes a Congressional Office of Regulatory Analysis
Requires the Office to conduct an assessment and analysis of any non-major rule upon request by a congressional committee or Member.
Document 2: Amends the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act
Makes this Act applicable to the provision of assistance in response to a major disaster or emergency.
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 133 Hare Road in Crosby, Texas, as the Army First Sergeant David McNerney Post Office Building.
Document 4: Declares that, in order to facilitate hydropower development on the Diamond Fork System
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to report to certain congressional committees.
Document 5: This bill amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to reauthorize through FY2021 a diesel emissions reduction program.
The program authorizes the Environmental Protection Agency to provide grants, rebates, or loans for reducing diesel emissions.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to repeal provisions prohibiting the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency from requiring permits for discharges of stormwater runoff.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to study and report on stormwater impacts.
Document 7: Amends title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize states to reserve school improvement funds.
Requires each LEA and school receiving school improvement funds to develop policies and practices for family engagement.
Document 8: Designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse located at 101 Barr Street in Lexington, Kentucky, as the 'Scott Reed Federal Building and United States Courthouse.'
Document 9: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 220 Elm Avenue in Munising, Michigan, as the 'Elizabeth L. Kinnunen Post Office Building.'
Document 10: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 115 4th Avenue Southwest in Ardmore, Oklahoma, as the 'Specialist Micheal E. Phillips Post Office.'

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at supporting and enhancing small businesses in the United States through funding, grants, and regulatory modifications.

Document 1: Requires federal contractors to comply with employment verification programs to support legal workforce participation.
Establishes a position to ensure SBA does not provide loans to unauthorized aliens.
Document 2: Directs the SBA to establish a grant program for small business development centers.
Funding is allocated for resources to assist struggling small businesses.
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to extend loan time limits for veteran-owned businesses.
Increases SBA funding for the Office of Veterans Business Development.
Document 7: Directs the SBA to develop entrepreneurial training content and a nationwide online network for small businesses.
Ensures timely and relevant content distribution to support entrepreneurial development.
Document 8: Amends tax code to provide a tax credit for equity investment in small businesses.
Specifies support for minority and women-owned small businesses.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, funding, and management of the United States Postal Service.

Document 1: Requires the United States Postal Service to issue and sell, at a premium, a Multinational Species Conservation Funds Semipostal Stamp.
Prohibits such proceeds from being taken into account in any decision relating to the level of appropriations or other federal funding to be furnished to the USFWS or such Funds.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to provide for a program under which postal benefits are provided to a member of the Armed Forces who is on active duty.
Makes the benefits program applicable during FY2014-FY2019.
Document 5: Modifies the procedures the U.S. Postal Service must follow in connection with the closing or consolidation of any postal facility.
Requires posted notice in each affected postal facility and notice by mail to all persons served by such postal facility at least 90 days before the final decision is made.
Document 6: Establishes in the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) the position of Chief Innovation Officer, who shall manage the development and implementation of innovative postal and nonpostal products and services.

Authorizes USPS to provide nonpostal services that use the processing, transportation, delivery, retail network, technology, or other resources of USPS in a manner consistent with the public interest.
Document 10: Prohibits the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) from closing or consolidating any postal facility without the approval of the Postal Regulatory Commission.
Before making a determination to close or consolidate a postal facility, the USPS must conduct an area mail processing study.

topic.name: Election Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at reforming election processes, including voting procedures, campaign financing, and redistricting transparency.

Document 1: Amends the Revised Statutes with respect to the time of election to establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as the days for the election.
Establishes the same polling place hours in the United States for both congressional and presidential elections.
Document 2: Requires states to carry out congressional redistricting in accordance with a process under which members of the public are informed of redistricting proposals.
Requires the state redistricting entity to solicit the input of members of the public in its work to develop initial congressional redistricting plans.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code and the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the system of public financing for presidential primary and general elections.
Requires presidential primary candidates who opt to participate in the public financing system to certify to the Federal Election Commission (FEC) that they have raised $25,000 in each of 20 states.
Document 4: Authorizes the State Elections Commission of Puerto Rico to provide for a ratification vote on the admission of Puerto Rico as a state of the United States.
Requires Congress to act, through legislation, to admit Puerto Rico as a state if a majority votes in favor.
Document 5: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require state and local election officials to consult with the U.S. Postal Service for absentee ballot design.
Requires a state to design each absentee ballot to include tracking information available to the voter before election day.
Document 6: Prohibits the use of federal funds received by any entity, other than a natural person, to advocate the election or defeat of a political candidate.
Exempts from this ban the use of funds by an entity for an activity specifically authorized by federal law.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements for Senate election campaigns.
Establishes a Senate Fair Elections Fund and modifies electioneering communication reporting requirements.

topic.name: Women's Health and Rights
topic.description: The documents collectively address issues related to women's health care access, reproductive rights, and protection against domestic violence.

Document 1: Expands the TRICARE health care program managed by the Department of Defense (DOD) to entitle additional female beneficiaries and dependents to care related to the prevention of pregnancy.
Prohibits cost-sharing from being imposed or collected for such pregnancy prevention care.
Document 3: This bill declares that the right to life guaranteed by the Constitution is vested in each human and is the person's paramount and most fundamental right.
Each human life begins with fertilization, cloning, or its functional equivalent, at which time every human has all legal and constitutional attributes and privileges of personhood.
Document 4: Directs a state to provide by law enhanced sentencing requirements for persons convicted of committing, or attempting to commit, an act of domestic violence in the presence of minor children.

Prohibits a state that fails within two years to implement this Act substantially from receiving 20% of the funds that would otherwise be allocated to it for the fiscal year under the Violence Against Women Act of 2000.
Document 5: This bill amends the federal criminal code to require any health care practitioner who is present when a child is born alive following an abortion or attempted abortion to exercise the same degree of care as reasonably provided to any other child born alive.
An individual who commits an overt act that kills a child born alive is subject to criminal prosecution for murder.
Document 6: Amends the Public Health Service Act to prohibit any recipient of a grant or contract under the family planning program from discriminating against any institutional or individual health care entity on the basis of whether such entity separately provides or refers for abortions.
Defines 'health care entity' to include an individual physician or other health care professional, a hospital, or any other kind of health care facility or organization.
Document 8: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require abortion providers, before a woman gives informed consent to any part of an abortion, to perform an obstetric ultrasound on the pregnant woman.
Prohibits construing this Act to require a woman to view the images or penalizing the provider or the woman if she declines to look at the images.

topic.name: Legislative Reforms and Governance
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at reforming governance structures, rights, and financial management in the United States and its territories.

Document 1: Amends the Inspector General Act of 1978 to repeal the authority of the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to appoint the Inspector General of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB).
Requires the CFPB Inspector General to be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.
Document 2: Considers the District of Columbia a congressional district for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives.
Applies to the District in the same manner as it applies to a state the federal law providing for the fifteenth and subsequent decennial censuses.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran is responsible for directing the commission of serious human rights abuses against Iranian citizens.

Amends the Comprehensive Iran Sanctions, Accountability, and Divestment Act of 2010 to require the President to investigate and determine within 180 days whether the person is such a described individual.
Document 4: Authorizes a bankruptcy court to establish a loss mitigation program for the negotiation of consensual alternatives to avoid foreclosure.
Establishes procedures for the negotiation of alternatives to avoid foreclosure between an individual debtor and the holder of a claim secured by a security interest.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to appoint personnel for health care positions within the Veterans Health Administration (VHA).
Removes or waives certain pay restrictions for physicians or dentists occupying administrative or executive leadership positions.
Document 6: Requires that the principal office of the U.S. Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims (CAVC) be in the metropolitan area of Washington, DC.
Sets forth exceptions for recall-eligible retired judges.
Document 7: Establishes the Puerto Rico Financial Stability Council, subject to enactment of a law by the Legislative Assembly and the governor of Puerto Rico.
The bill prescribes procedures for submission to the Council of revised financial plans or revised budgets in the event of disapproval.
Document 8: Amends federal law concerning the leasing to private entities by the Secretary of the Interior of certain federal oil shale reserves in Colorado.
States that 50% of proceeds shall be transferred from the Secretary of the Treasury to the state of Colorado for deposit into its oil shale special fund.
Document 9: Amends the Professional Boxing Safety Act of 1996 to authorize a tribal organization to establish a boxing commission.
Establishes the USBC within the Department of Commerce to protect the health, safety, and general interests of boxers.
Document 10: Amends the federal bankruptcy code to treat Puerto Rico as a state under chapter 9.
Provides for adjustments of the debts of municipalities.

topic.name: Federal Assistance Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal legislative acts aimed at regulating, funding, and improving assistance programs across housing, education, environmental restoration, and military benefits.

Document 1: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to apply to a family residing in a dwelling unit covered by a contract for project-based rental assistance under section 8.
requirements for treatment of families whose welfare or public assistance benefits are reduced.
Document 2: requires that the website of the Office of Management and Budget include a program inventory that identifies each program of the federal government for which there is more than $1 million in annual budget authority.
description of the individuals served by a program and beneficiaries who received financial assistance under a program.
Document 3: allocates funding to coastal states and affected Indian tribes,
make grants to regional ocean and coastal planning bodies.
Document 4: establish a new part D, entitled 'the Promise Neighborhoods Act of 2015.'
to award renewable, competitive, matching grants to institutions of higher education.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Defense to determine whether former members of the Armed Forces who were separated for a violation of the policy concerning homosexuality were paid the full amount of separation pay.
Requires the Secretary to pay such a former member who has not received the full military separation pay.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the city all interest of the United States in such land in exchange for consideration in an amount equal to the fair market value of the land.
Requires the Secretary to determine the fair market value of the federal land based upon an appraisal.
Document 7: Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Agency to direct Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to issue guidance providing that the levy of a PACE assessment does not constitute a default on any loan.
Prohibits federal agencies from discriminating against state or local governments implementing or participating in a PACE program.
Document 8: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 with respect to the requirement that the Department of Housing and Urban Development require local governments to make inspections and rent determinations.
A dwelling unit shall not be deemed to be owned by a PHA for these purposes.
Document 9: Establishes the Gulf Coast Ecosystem Restoration Task Force, which shall consult with, and provide recommendations regarding approval of State Coastal Ecosystem Restoration Plans.
Makes the Fund available for the conservation, protection, and restoration of the Gulf Coast ecosystem.
Document 10: Authorizes certain members of the Armed Forces to make an irrevocable election to become entitled to basic educational assistance under the Montgomery GI Bill.
Provides for notice of the opportunity created by this Act to elect educational assistance.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at regulating and improving health care access, insurance coverage, and health equity in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) from using any recommendation of the Preventive Services Task Force to deny or restrict coverage of an item or service under a federal health care program.

require the sponsor of a group health plan, in determining which preventive items and services to provide coverage for, to consult the medical guidelines and recommendations of relevant professional medical organizations.
Document 3: Prohibits an employer from enrolling an employee in the CLASS program without providing specified notice to the employee.
Establishes a refund process.
Document 4: Prohibit a group health plan from imposing any preexisting conditions exclusion.
Requires each health insurance issuer that offers health insurance coverage in the group market in a state to accept every employer in the state that applies for such coverage.
Document 5: expanding the collection and analysis of data in programs of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS).
HHS must engage in activities regarding health workforce diversity.
Document 6: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to health savings accounts (HSAs) to allow spouses to make increased catch-up contributions to a single HSA.
Treats as medical care for purposes of the tax deduction for medical expenses certain exercise equipment and physical fitness programs.
Document 7: Repeals the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act and the health care provisions of the Health Care and Education and Reconciliation Act of 2010.
Amends the Public Health Service Act to provide for the establishment and governance of individual membership associations.
Document 8: This bill expresses the sense of the Senate that Congress must address the unfair exclusion of professional and hardworking home care providers.
This bill amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act.
Document 9: establishes an independent Commission to Improve Labor Statistics.
Directs the Commission to examine and make an assessment of the process by which the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) collects, processes, analyzes, and disseminates statistical data.
Document 10: Defines "covered entity" as a person engaged in interstate commerce that collects or stores data containing covered or sensitive information.
Requires a covered entity to make available to individuals whose information it collects or maintains information about its information privacy practices.

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Accountability
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing legal accountability and procedural fairness across multiple sectors, including government contracting, criminal justice, and financial regulation.

1: permit the debarment or suspension of U.S. government contractors for evading service of process
require such contractors performing federal contracts of $5 million or more outside the United States to consent to personal jurisdiction
2: allow any person on trial for a federal marijuana-related offense to introduce evidence that the alleged marijuana-related activities were performed in compliance with state law
limit the criminal liability of persons convicted of federal marijuana-related offenses
3: prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from granting a work authorization to an alien who has been determined, in a valid immigration proceeding, to have been at any time unlawfully present in the United States
4: require a federal financial institutions regulatory agency to make a final examination report to a financial institution within 60 days
grants a financial institution the right to appeal a material supervisory determination contained in a final report of examination
5: requires the Federal Communications Commission to develop and implement a program to provide for the continued broadcasting in the analog television service of specified public safety information
6: defines the treason, bribery, or other high crimes and misdemeanors for which a justice or judge of the United States shall be removed from office
7: grant performers of sound recordings equal rights to compensation from terrestrial broadcasters
prohibits license fees payable for the public performance of sound recordings from being used in any governmental proceeding
8: authorize the Attorney General to obtain electronic versions of information from federal agencies on persons disqualified from receiving firearms
requires federal agencies to provide such information to the Attorney General, not less frequently than quarterly
9: permits federal law enforcement officials to seek an ex parte order in federal court for the disclosure of tax returns or return information for criminal investigations pertaining to missing or exploited children
may disclose to state or local law enforcement agencies information obtained under the authority provided by this bill
10: require the attorney for the government, in a criminal prosecution, to provide to the defendant any information or evidence that may reasonably appear to be favorable to the defendant
directs the government attorney to provide to the defendant any covered information without delay after arraignment

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Public Safety and Services
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing public safety, emergency services, and community support mechanisms.

Document 1: Improving Assistance to Domestic and Sexual Violence Victims Act of 2009
Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 to revise definitions under such Act
Document 4: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1986 to include within the definitions of 'member of a rescue squad or ambulance crew'
is a public agency or a nonprofit entity serving the public that is officially authorized or licensed to engage in rescue activity or to provide emergency medical services
Document 5: Prohibits a state or local government from receiving federal financial assistance for a minimum of one year if such jurisdiction restricts or prohibits a government entity or official from: (1) sending to or receiving from the responsible federal immigration agency information regarding an individual's citizenship or immigration status
Restores assistance eligibility upon a determination by the Attorney General (DOJ) that the jurisdiction no longer restricts or prohibits such actions

topic.name: Legislative Reforms and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming government processes, enhancing transparency, and providing oversight in areas such as civil forfeiture, election spending, budgeting, and environmental management.

Document 1: modifies general rules governing civil forfeiture proceedings
requires proceeds from the disposition of seized property to be deposited into the General Fund of the Treasury
Document 2: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971
prescribe disclosure requirements for campaign-related disbursements
Document 3: requires biennial budget resolutions
requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to determine the impact and feasibility of changing the definition of a fiscal year
Document 4: establish an amount that is equal to 95% of the full funding amount used for the previous fiscal year
permits a resource advisory committee to propose using up to 10% of certain project funds
Document 5: requires the Secretary of Commerce to establish criteria for determining which states should serve on the Inter-Agency Task Force
promoting a national strategy to help communities understand, detect, predict, control, and mitigate freshwater and marine hypoxia events
Document 6: renames the Department of Defense regional center for security studies
Document 7: directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to carry out a program of outreach to veterans
authorizes the Secretary to enter into agreements with federal and state agencies
Document 8: establish a national Silver Alert communications network
define 'missing senior' as any individual who is reported as missing
Document 9: directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey three parcels of Bureau of Land Management land
requires the federal land to be used according to the agreement
Document 10: defines specified terms related to commodity programs
directs the Secretary of Agriculture to adjust base acres for covered commodities

topic.name: Environmental and Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to environmental protection, infrastructure development, and resource management in the United States.

Document 1: Great Smoky Mountains National Park Agreement Act of 2014
transfer specified funds previously appropriated to the National Park Service
Document 4: Great Lakes Icebreaker Replacement Act
necessary expenses of the Coast Guard relating to the design, acquisition, and construction
Document 5: Great Lakes Navigation System Sustainability Act of 2013
manage and allocate funding for all commercial and recreational navigation projects
Document 10: Defending Our Great Lakes Act of 2015
prevent the upstream transfer of aquatic nuisance species from the Mississippi River basin to the Great Lakes basin
